trivia_bullets.text: The card's name and art reflect a core narrative thread of Battle for Zendikar: the Gatewatch and their allies discovering that the hedron network and leylines of Zendikar were the key to defeating the Eldrazi titans. Jori En's expeditionary research — finding alien Eldrazi-scarred coastlines and tidal zones — is one of the storyline's primary knowledge-recovery arcs. [Wizards story: 'The Believers' Pilgrimage', 2015-09-02] [Jori En via WebSearch: coolstuffinc.com 'Legends of Zendikar']

The artist, Kieran Yanner, painted Coastal Discovery as a freelance illustrator in 2015. He subsequently joined Wizards of the Coast in February 2017 as Senior Art Director, sharing responsibilities formerly held by Jeremy Jarvis, alongside Dawn Murin, Cynthia Sheppard, and Mark Winters. He held the role until December 2021. Coastal Discovery thus predates Yanner's tenure as the person who would have directed other artists' work for the same game. [MTG Wiki: Kieran Yanner via WebSearch]

Coastal Discovery is a {3}{U} sorcery that draws two cards, with an Awaken 4 mode at {5}{U} that also animates a land with four +1/+1 counters. Scryfall confirms no flavor text on the card. It is not legal in Pauper (uncommon) and carries an EDHREC rank of 21372, reflecting low Commander adoption — likely displaced by cheaper or more efficient draw spells at the same slot. [Scryfall]

The four-counter Awaken 4 mode produces a 4/4 land Elemental — among the larger land creatures in the BFZ awaken cycle. The 0/0-plus-counters rules framing means a second awaken cast on the same land stacks: two awaken 4 hits produce an 8/8. This edge-case stacking was explicitly called out in the official BFZ mechanics article as a feature, not a bug. [magic.wizards.com: Battle for Zendikar Mechanics]
